title: Three.js WebGL Renderer Lite
lead_source: Public GitHub search found a May-updated non-bounty issue asking for a simple WebGL renderer for Three.js that the requester can pay for: https://github.com/RenaudRohlinger/webgl-compute-pbo/issues/1. No external comment was posted.
acceptance_statement: I accept the Three.js WebGL Renderer Lite fixed-scope terms at $100. I understand work starts only after seller-owned external payment proof exists; I will provide only public or buyer-authorized non-sensitive rendering requirements; the deliverable is limited to a simple browser-renderable Three.js/WebGL scene, renderer setup notes, and integration checklist; and custom engine work, asset licensing, private repo access, credentials, performance guarantees, ongoing support, or extra revisions are not included unless separately agreed before payment.
response_draft_not_posted: I saw this request and can offer a narrow fixed-scope Three.js WebGL Renderer Lite pass for $100. I would start with public or buyer-authorized non-sensitive details only, confirm the exact browser-renderable scene and acceptance criteria first, then use a seller-owned payment route only after scope is accepted. I would not need credentials, private payment data, private repo access, or confidential files in the public issue.
posting_boundary: The draft response is prepared but not posted. External public comments or direct outreach require explicit send approval.
money_rule: Count $0 until real buyer acceptance, seller-owned external payment proof, delivery proof, and posted/released/payable/cleared funds exist.
